description: The worm: the soil's quiet maker, turning earth as it goes. Built on the 'mu' of 'muila' (earth), like 'muholi' (dig) — the earth-family's smallest member and hardest worker.
sound_symbolism: 'mu' is the soil itself, 'ri' the small movement through it, 'we' the soft body doing the moving. The earth-family signature carried by its tiniest bearer.
grammatical_notes: Noun: 'lo muriwe muila temoe.' (The worms tend the earth.) In the garden: 'thepalu lo muriwe phelona.' (The garden requires worms.)

pillars.solarpunk-values: Soil health is worm health; the regenerative future is built, quite literally, by this animal. Honoring it by name is agricultural realism.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Farmers judged a field by its worms long before soil science agreed with them.
